name: Amazon
description: American multinational technology and e-commerce company operating online retail, cloud computing (AWS), and consumer devices.

years.notes: Smile/arrow wordmark introduced 25 January 2000 by Turner Duckworth — 'amazon.com' lowercase with the curved orange arrow from 'a' to 'z'. Used through 2012. Wordmark scaled to fit 24×24 viewBox (text renders very small at icon sizes — cataloging tradeoff).

Dropped the '.com' suffix in 2012 — wordmark simplified to 'amazon' over the same smile arrow. Used through April 2024. Wordmark scaled to fit 24×24 viewBox (text renders very small at icon sizes — cataloging tradeoff).

Refresh released 15 April 2024 — new Amazon Ember Modern wordmark (Koto + NaN Foundry), slightly thicker smile and a deeper 'Smile Orange' (#FF6201) replacing the classic #FF9900. Wordmark scaled to fit 24×24 viewBox (text renders very small at icon sizes — cataloging tradeoff).

notes: Skipped millésimes: 1995 original 'A-river' mark by Turner Duckworth (only low-res raster references on logo-history sites — no authoritative true-vector SVG on Wikimedia Commons or archive.org), 1997 'zebra' refresh and 1998 'amazon.com' serif + 'swish' variants (same — no vector source). Per the no-invent rule these millésimes were skipped. `latest` set to 2024 — current Amazon Ember Modern wordmark with the Smile Orange (#FF6201) palette.
